phase-plan.purpose: Make the public site operational by turning submissions, scheduling, and quotes into managed CRM relationships with EngageBay as the default provider and HubSpot as a supported fallback.
phase-plan.scope: In scope: form-to-contact sync, first-party scheduling forms, call and quote intake handoff, CRM provider abstraction, runtime provider selection, and quote summary sync into CRM deals. Out of scope: customer-visible quote acceptance, accounting sync, and bidirectional CRM ownership unless explicitly approved later.

file.description: Public-site conversion flow and content checklist.
Quote workflow and CRM handoff planning.

task.description: Keep every public lead tied to the active CRM provider with clear sync status and task ownership, and move quote/contact/schedule capture onto a shared first-party form system.
Make scheduling part of the lead pipeline with a first-party form flow, shared validators/helpers, and Payload-to-CRM write-back instead of an embedded vendor form.
Mirror Payload quotes into CRM deals so follow-up and relationship management happen in the chosen provider.

questions.question: Should v1 treat Payload quotes as the source of truth and push one-way to CRM deals, or allow CRM stage changes to write back later?
How should new leads and synced deals be assigned to an owner in the active CRM provider for follow-up?
Is the current EngageBay 403 coming from trial API-call limits, the wrong key scope, or a throttling window that needs retry logic?
